---
title: "Developer Guide (v0.0.1)"
---

# Developer Guide — yrS3 v0.0.1 {#developer-guide}

This document gives an overview of everything a developer on this project needs to know: what the system is, how the repo is organized, how to run and deploy it, and where to find or extend the code and documentation.

**Related:** For documentation types and version numbering, see [DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd](DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd). For full MVP scope and behaviour, see [SPEC-0.0.1.qmd](SPEC-0.0.1.qmd).

---

## What is yrS3? {#sec-what-is-yrs3}

**yrS3** is a web-based **tutorial and testing system** for knowledge assessment. Version **0.0.1** focuses on the **testing component**:

- **Learners** log in, open **question sets** (from DB or file), take a set as a test, answer questions (multiple choice, short answer, long answer, R code, Bash), submit, and receive scores and feedback.
- **Users** can create and upload new questions or modifications; questions live in a filesystem (or Supabase Storage) with one folder per question (YAML + prompt). Any user can propose a new version of any question; the version is **pending** until the question owner approves it.
- **Auth** is email/password (with email confirmation) via Supabase; optional Google and GitHub OAuth are supported and documented separately.

The full product vision is in the PRD documents; the **SPEC** defines the initial MVP scope and technical behaviour.

---

## Tech stack {#sec-tech-stack}

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS |
| **Auth & optional DB** | Supabase (auth; optional database and storage) |
| **Deploy** | Vercel |
| **Requirements** | Node.js 20+ |

Questions use the **database** as the source of truth (ownership, status, listing). Content is stored on the **filesystem** (local dev) or in **Supabase Storage** (e.g. on Vercel); when using FS, the app can mirror DB metadata in an optional `db_meta.yaml` per version and sync FS with DB (import FS-only, DB wins on conflict). See [SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd](SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd). Code execution for R/Bash questions uses a sandbox; see [SPEC-0.0.1.qmd](SPEC-0.0.1.qmd) for constraints.

---

## Repository structure {#sec-repo-structure}

```
yrS3/
├── src/
│   ├── app/                    # Next.js App Router: pages and API routes
│   │   ├── api/                # API routes (questions, question-sets, run-code, auth)
│   │   ├── auth/               # Auth callback
│   │   ├── author/             # Upload questions (file-based)
│   │   ├── login/              # Login page
│   │   ├── question-sets/      # Question set list, create, take-as-test flow
│   │   ├── tests/              # Redirect to question-sets
│   │   ├── layout.tsx, page.tsx, globals.css
│   ├── lib/                    # Shared logic
│   │   ├── auth.ts             # Auth helpers
│   │   ├── questions/          # Question types, parse, validate, store (fs + Supabase)
│   │   ├── evaluators/         # Grading: multiple-choice, short-answer, bash, etc.
│   │   ├── sandbox.ts          # Code execution interface
│   │   ├── supabase/           # Supabase client and server utilities
│   │   ├── question-sets/      # Question set types, load from DB + filesystem
│   │   ├── logger.ts           # Audit logging
│   ├── middleware.ts           # Auth middleware
├── docs/                       # Quarto book source (all .qmd files)
│   ├── _quarto.yml             # Book config and chapter list
│   ├── index.qmd, DOC-GUIDE, SPEC, SETUP, QUESTION-FORMAT, PRD, etc.
├── questions/                  # Default root for question folders (local)
├── question-sets/              # Optional root for file-based question sets (set.yaml per folder)
├── supabase/
│   ├── config.toml             # Local Supabase auth/config
│   └── migrations/             # SQL migrations (question_sets, question_set_items, test_sessions, answers, question_versions, audit_logs, storage)
├── .env.example                # Copy to .env.local and fill in
├── package.json, tsconfig.json, vercel.json
└── README.md                   # Project overview (single source for GitHub + book)
```

- **App entry:** `src/app/` — routes and API handlers.
- **Business logic:** `src/lib/` — questions, question-sets (DB + file), evaluators, auth, Supabase, logging.
- **Docs:** `docs/` — all documentation; build the book with `quarto render` from `docs/`.
- **Questions (local):** `questions/` — one folder per question; see [QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd](QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd).

---

## Key documentation {#sec-key-docs}

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd](DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd) | How to navigate docs and version numbering |
| [SPEC-0.0.1.qmd](SPEC-0.0.1.qmd) | MVP scope, behaviour, and technical spec |
| [SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2.qmd](SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2.qmd) | Question sets (DB + file-based), take as test |
| [SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd](SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd) | DB–FS question sync, dual-write, backup, and `db_meta.yaml` |
| [QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd](QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd) | File-based question set layout (`set.yaml`) |
| [SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd](SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd) | Install dependencies, env vars, local run, Supabase (local + hosted), deploy to Vercel |
| [QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd](QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd) | Question folder layout, `meta.yaml`, prompt file, type-specific files, validation rules |
| PRD-0.0.1-v\*.qmd | Product vision and full requirements |
| [QUARTO-DOC-GUIDE.qmd](QUARTO-DOC-GUIDE.qmd) | Install Quarto and render the docs book |
| [DOC-AUTHORING-GUIDE.qmd](DOC-AUTHORING-GUIDE.qmd) | How to write and maintain docs (`.qmd`, cross-refs, book) |

The rendered HTML book is in `docs/_book/` after running `quarto render` from `docs/`.

---

## Getting started (local) {#sec-getting-started}

1. **Clone and install**

   ```bash
   git clone <repo-url>
   cd yrS3
   npm install
   ```

2. **Environment**

   ```bash
   cp .env.example .env.local
   ```

   Set in `.env.local`:

   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` — Supabase project URL
   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — Supabase anon key

   For local Supabase: run `supabase start`, then `supabase status -o env` and copy the URL and anon key. See [SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd](SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd) for full steps (including email confirmation and optional OAuth).

3. **Questions directory (filesystem mode)**

   ```bash
   mkdir -p questions
   ```

   Add at least one question (see [QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd](QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd)); the SETUP guide has a minimal multiple-choice example.

4. **Question sets and sync**

   Question sets are loaded from the database and/or from the filesystem (see [QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd](QUESTION-SET-FORMAT-0.0.2.qmd)). Optionally set `QUESTION_SETS_ROOT` to a path for file-based sets; default is `./question-sets`. When using FS for questions, set `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` (a user UUID) so that FS-only question versions are imported into the DB on first list; see [SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd](SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd).

5. **Run**

   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

   Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000). Sign in (after confirming email if using email/password), then open question sets (take as test) or create question sets and questions.

---

## Environment variables {#sec-env-vars}

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | Yes | — | Supabase project URL |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Yes | — | Supabase anon key |
| `QUESTIONS_STORAGE` | No | (filesystem) | Set to `supabase` for production (e.g. Vercel) |
| `QUESTIONS_ROOT` | No | `./questions` | Local path for question folders |
| `QUESTIONS_BUCKET` | No | `questions` | Supabase Storage bucket when `QUESTIONS_STORAGE=supabase` |
| `QUESTION_SETS_ROOT` | No | `./question-sets` | Local path for file-based question sets (set.yaml per folder) |
| `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` | No | — | When using FS for questions: UUID of user to assign when importing FS-only versions into the DB (required for sync import) |
| `QUESTIONS_FS_BACKUP_ROOT` | No | — | When `QUESTIONS_STORAGE=supabase`: local path to mirror each version for backup (content + db_meta.yaml) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` | No | — | App URL for redirects |

Full details and deployment notes: [SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd](SETUP-0.0.1-v001.qmd#sec-env-vars).

---

## App architecture (code) {#sec-architecture}

- **Routing:** Next.js App Router under `src/app/`. Pages: `/`, `/login`, `/questions`, `/questions/new`, `/author`, `/question-sets`, `/question-sets/new`, `/question-sets/[id]/take`. Legacy `/tests` redirects to `/question-sets`. API routes under `src/app/api/`.
- **Auth:** Supabase Auth; middleware in `src/middleware.ts`; helpers in `src/lib/auth.ts`; client/server Supabase in `src/lib/supabase/`.
- **Questions:** Loaded and validated via `src/lib/questions/` (types, parse, validate). The **database** is the source of truth for listing and ownership; storage (FS or Supabase) holds content. `store-fs.ts`, `store-supabase.ts`, and `get-store.ts` abstract storage; `store-db.ts` and `sync-questions.ts` handle DB and FS–DB sync. On create/update/approve, the app dual-writes to the DB and to the FS (optional `db_meta.yaml` per version for ownership/status). When using FS and `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID`, `GET /api/questions` runs sync once (import FS-only, DB wins on conflict) then lists from the DB. See SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC.
- **Evaluators:** `src/lib/evaluators/` — one module per question type (multiple-choice, short-answer, bash, etc.); each returns score/feedback in a consistent shape. See SPEC for which types are in MVP.
- **Sandbox:** `src/lib/sandbox.ts` — interface for running user code (e.g. Bash); used by the run-code API and evaluators. SPEC defines isolation and logging requirements.
- **Question sets:** `src/lib/question-sets/` loads sets from the DB and/or filesystem (QUESTION-SET-FORMAT). Create sets via UI or add `set.yaml` under `question-sets/`. Taking a set as a test creates a test session (table `test_sessions` with `set_id` referencing `question_sets.id`); answers stored in `answers`.
- **Logging:** `src/lib/logger.ts` — structured audit logs (auth, uploads, test actions, sandbox runs). SPEC defines scope; retention is deployment-specific.

---

## Question format and creation {#sec-question-format}

- **One folder per question;** folder name SHOULD be the question `id`.
- **Required:** `meta.yaml` (id, type, version) and exactly one of `prompt.md` or `prompt.txt` (prompt.md wins if both exist).
- **Type-specific:** e.g. `options.yaml` for multiple choice, `solution.sh`/`solution.R` for bash/R, `expected.yaml` or `rubric.yaml` for short/long answer. See [QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd](QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd) for all types, required files, and edge cases.
- **Validation:** Upload API and loaders MUST follow QUESTION-FORMAT (required fields, prompt rule, type-specific files, multiple-choice correctness). Invalid uploads are rejected with clear errors.
- **Questions:** Any authenticated user can create questions and propose new versions. Proposed versions by non-owners are **pending** until the question owner approves them. Only approved versions are used in tests.

---

## Database (Supabase) {#sec-database}

Migrations live in `supabase/migrations/`. The schema includes:

- **question_sets** — id, title, description, created_at (question set definitions).
- **question_set_items** — question_set_id, question_logical_id, order (items in a set).
- **test_sessions** — user_id, set_id (references question_sets.id), attempt_number, started_at, submitted_at, score.
- **answers** — session_id, question_id, question_version, answer_text, evaluation_json, score, timestamps, keystroke_metrics.
- **audit_logs** — category, event, user_id, payload (structured logging).
- **question_versions** — logical_id, version, owner_id, type, title, domain, storage_path, status (pending/approved), proposed_by, created_at. Source of truth for listing and ownership; content in storage. Any authenticated user can insert; owner can update (e.g. approve) and delete. Only approved versions are "official."
- **Storage bucket** `questions` and RLS for read/write by authenticated users.

Apply migrations: `supabase db push` (hosted) or `supabase db reset` (local). RLS policies restrict access per SPEC.

---

## Documentation (Quarto book) {#sec-docs-book}

- All doc source files are in `docs/` as `.qmd` (Quarto) files.
- Book config and chapter order: `docs/_quarto.yml`. To add a new chapter, add its filename under `book:` → `chapters:` (or under a `part`).
- Build the book from the `docs/` directory: `quarto render`. Output: `docs/_book/` (open `index.html` in a browser).
- Writing conventions: one top-level `#` per doc, use `{#sec-id}` on headings and `@sec-id` for cross-references, no hardcoded section numbers. See [DOC-AUTHORING-GUIDE.qmd](DOC-AUTHORING-GUIDE.qmd).

---

## Deployment {#sec-deployment}

- **Vercel:** Connect the repo or use `vercel` CLI; set env vars (including `QUESTIONS_STORAGE=supabase` for production). See SETUP for Vercel CLI and Dashboard options.
- **Supabase (production):** Use a hosted project; set Site URL and Redirect URLs in the Dashboard to the production app URL. Migrations applied via `supabase db push` for the linked project.
- **Separate environments:** Use different Supabase projects and Vercel envs (Production / Preview) for prod vs staging; SETUP describes the mapping.

---

## Where to extend and open items {#sec-extend}

- **New question type:** Add type in QUESTION-FORMAT and SPEC; implement evaluator in `src/lib/evaluators/` and wire it into the submission flow; add any type-specific validation in `src/lib/questions/validate.ts`.
- **Sandbox:** SPEC leaves the exact implementation (container, serverless, or service) open; implement behind `src/lib/sandbox.ts` and document security and logging.
- **API contracts:** SPEC lists open items: finalize API contracts for upload, test listing, and submission; define evaluator output schema; decide where student progress lives first (DB only, filesystem, or both); define how tests are composed (e.g. config or folder).
- **Log format and retention:** SPEC specifies scope; format, storage, and retention are MVP/deployment decisions.

For the full list of open items and TODOs, see the end of [SPEC-0.0.1.qmd](SPEC-0.0.1.qmd).

---

## Summary {#sec-summary}

- **yrS3** is a tutorial and testing app: learners take tests, users create and upload questions; auth via Supabase, deploy on Vercel.
- **Code:** Next.js in `src/app/` and `src/lib/`; questions in `questions/` or Supabase Storage; DB and auth in Supabase.
- **Docs:** All in `docs/`; build with `quarto render`; add chapters in `_quarto.yml`.
- **Run locally:** `npm install`, `.env.local` with Supabase URL and key, optional `questions/` and `question-sets/` (file-based sets), `npm run dev`.
- **Deep dives:** SPEC (scope and behaviour), SETUP (install and deploy), QUESTION-FORMAT (question layout and rules), DOC-GUIDE and DOC-AUTHORING-GUIDE (docs and versioning).
